qa_correction_applied: BUG3 Technical Hints corrected per ba-qa-report-20260524-125300-A.json issue I1: _first_word(after) returns '|' not 'bash'; correct approach is any(_first_word(seg) in _SHELL_INTERPS for seg in after.split('|')[1:])

acceptance_criteria.title: Combined flag -lc exposes rm
acceptance_criteria.given: The function strip_non_executable_contexts is called with the string "bash -lc 'rm /tmp/x'"
acceptance_criteria.when: strip_non_executable_contexts processes the combined short-option word -lc
acceptance_criteria.then: The returned string matches grep -qE '(^|[ \t;|&(])rm\s' — rm is visible as executable text and not hidden inside a quoted token
acceptance_criteria.fix_location: hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py:298 — replace text.strip() == "-c" with a check that the token starts with - or + (not --) AND 'c' in text.strip()[1:]
acceptance_criteria.verification_command: python3 -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0, 'hooks/lib'); from bash_context_strip import strip_non_executable_contexts; import re; out = strip_non_executable_contexts(\"bash -lc 'rm /tmp/x'\"); assert re.search(r'(^|[ \t;|&(])rm\s', out), f'rm not visible in: {out!r}'; print('AC1 PASS:', repr(out))"

acceptance_criteria.title: Combined flag -ce exposes rm
acceptance_criteria.given: The function strip_non_executable_contexts is called with the string "bash -ce 'rm /tmp/x'"
acceptance_criteria.when: strip_non_executable_contexts processes the combined short-option word -ce
acceptance_criteria.then: The returned string matches grep -qE '(^|[ \t;|&(])rm\s' — rm is visible as executable text
acceptance_criteria.fix_location: hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py:298 — same fix as AC1; -ce contains 'c' at index 1 of the option cluster
acceptance_criteria.verification_command: python3 -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0, 'hooks/lib'); from bash_context_strip import strip_non_executable_contexts; import re; out = strip_non_executable_contexts(\"bash -ce 'rm /tmp/x'\"); assert re.search(r'(^|[ \t;|&(])rm\s', out), f'rm not visible in: {out!r}'; print('AC2 PASS:', repr(out))"

acceptance_criteria.title: sudo -u root shifts to bash — rm exposed
acceptance_criteria.given: The function strip_non_executable_contexts is called with the string "sudo -u root bash -c 'rm /tmp/x'"
acceptance_criteria.when: strip_non_executable_contexts processes the wrapper prefix sudo followed by the option-argument pair -u root
acceptance_criteria.then: The returned string matches grep -qE '(^|[ \t;|&(])rm\s' — rm is visible as executable text; the function correctly identifies bash as the command word after consuming -u root
acceptance_criteria.fix_location: hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py:219-222 — after detecting word in _WRAPPER_PREFIXES, add a loop consuming option tokens: if token in _SUDO_OPTS_WITH_ARG skip token and next token; if token == '--' skip and stop; if token starts with - or + (not --) skip only the flag
acceptance_criteria.verification_command: python3 -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0, 'hooks/lib'); from bash_context_strip import strip_non_executable_contexts; import re; out = strip_non_executable_contexts(\"sudo -u root bash -c 'rm /tmp/x'\"); assert re.search(r'(^|[ \t;|&(])rm\s', out), f'rm not visible in: {out!r}'; print('AC3 PASS:', repr(out))"

acceptance_criteria.title: sudo -H (no-argument flag) does not over-skip — rm exposed
acceptance_criteria.given: The function strip_non_executable_contexts is called with the string "sudo -H bash -c 'rm /tmp/x'"
acceptance_criteria.when: strip_non_executable_contexts processes sudo followed by -H (a no-argument flag) and then bash
acceptance_criteria.then: The returned string matches grep -qE '(^|[ \t;|&(])rm\s' — rm is visible; the fix does not over-skip by treating bash as the argument to -H
acceptance_criteria.fix_location: hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py:219-222 — -H must be handled as a no-argument flag (skip flag only, not the following token); _SUDO_OPTS_WITH_ARG must NOT include -H
acceptance_criteria.verification_command: python3 -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0, 'hooks/lib'); from bash_context_strip import strip_non_executable_contexts; import re; out = strip_non_executable_contexts(\"sudo -H bash -c 'rm /tmp/x'\"); assert re.search(r'(^|[ \t;|&(])rm\s', out), f'rm not visible in: {out!r}'; print('AC4 PASS:', repr(out))"

acceptance_criteria.title: sudo -- terminates options — rm exposed
acceptance_criteria.given: The function strip_non_executable_contexts is called with the string "sudo -- bash -c 'rm /tmp/x'"
acceptance_criteria.when: strip_non_executable_contexts processes sudo followed by -- (option terminator) and then bash
acceptance_criteria.then: The returned string matches grep -qE '(^|[ \t;|&(])rm\s' — rm is visible; -- causes immediate stop of option parsing and the next token (bash) is recognised as the command word
acceptance_criteria.fix_location: hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py:219-222 — when the token is --, skip it and break out of the option-consuming loop immediately; the next token is the command word
acceptance_criteria.verification_command: python3 -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0, 'hooks/lib'); from bash_context_strip import strip_non_executable_contexts; import re; out = strip_non_executable_contexts(\"sudo -- bash -c 'rm /tmp/x'\"); assert re.search(r'(^|[ \t;|&(])rm\s', out), f'rm not visible in: {out!r}'; print('AC5 PASS:', repr(out))"

acceptance_criteria.title: Heredoc piped to /bin/bash preserves body
acceptance_criteria.given: The function strip_non_executable_contexts is called with the multi-line string "cat <<'EOF' | /bin/bash\nrm /tmp/x\nEOF"
acceptance_criteria.when: strip_non_executable_contexts processes the heredoc whose consumer line contains | /bin/bash
acceptance_criteria.then: The returned string contains 'rm /tmp/x' — the heredoc body is preserved as executable text, not zeroed out
acceptance_criteria.fix_location: hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py:353 — replace or augment shell_ctx detection. The after variable contains ' | /bin/bash'; _first_word(after) returns '|' (WRONG — pipe is tokenised as a word). Correct approach: any(_first_word(seg) in _SHELL_INTERPS for seg in after.split('|')[1:]) — splits after on '|', skips the segment before the first pipe, and checks _first_word of each subsequent segment
acceptance_criteria.fix_note: QA correction (ba-qa-report I1): _first_word(after) returns '|' not 'bash'. The correct implementation is any(_first_word(seg) in _SHELL_INTERPS for seg in after.split('|')[1:])
acceptance_criteria.verification_command: python3 -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0, 'hooks/lib'); from bash_context_strip import strip_non_executable_contexts; cmd = \"cat <<'EOF' | /bin/bash\nrm /tmp/x\nEOF\"; out = strip_non_executable_contexts(cmd); assert 'rm /tmp/x' in out, f'heredoc body not preserved in: {out!r}'; print('AC6 PASS:', repr(out))"

acceptance_criteria.title: Heredoc piped to /usr/bin/bash preserves body
acceptance_criteria.given: The function strip_non_executable_contexts is called with the multi-line string "cat <<'EOF' | /usr/bin/bash\nkill -9 1234\nEOF"
acceptance_criteria.when: strip_non_executable_contexts processes the heredoc whose consumer line contains | /usr/bin/bash
acceptance_criteria.then: The returned string matches grep -qE '(^|[ \t;|&])(kill)[ \t]+-' — kill is visible as executable text; /usr/bin/bash basename resolves to bash which is in _SHELL_INTERPS
acceptance_criteria.fix_location: hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py:353 — same fix as AC6; _first_word('/usr/bin/bash') = 'bash' (via _basename), so any(_first_word(seg) in _SHELL_INTERPS for seg in after.split('|')[1:]) correctly detects /usr/bin/bash
acceptance_criteria.fix_note: QA correction (ba-qa-report I1): after.split('|')[1:] yields [' /usr/bin/bash']; _first_word(' /usr/bin/bash') calls _basename and returns 'bash'; 'bash' in _SHELL_INTERPS is True
acceptance_criteria.verification_command: python3 -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0, 'hooks/lib'); from bash_context_strip import strip_non_executable_contexts; import re; cmd = \"cat <<'EOF' | /usr/bin/bash\nkill -9 1234\nEOF\"; out = strip_non_executable_contexts(cmd); assert re.search(r'(^|[ \t;|&])(kill)[ \t]+-', out), f'kill not visible in: {out!r}'; print('AC7 PASS:', repr(out))"

acceptance_criteria.title: Non-shell absolute path in pipe does NOT preserve body
acceptance_criteria.given: The function strip_non_executable_contexts is called with the multi-line string "cat <<'EOF' | /tmp/bash.old\nrm /tmp/x\nEOF"
acceptance_criteria.when: strip_non_executable_contexts processes the heredoc whose consumer line contains | /tmp/bash.old
acceptance_criteria.then: The returned string does NOT match grep -qE '(^|[ \t;|&(])rm\s' — basename 'bash.old' is not in _SHELL_INTERPS; the heredoc body is stripped (treated as non-shell context)
acceptance_criteria.fix_location: hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py:353 — the any(_first_word(seg) in _SHELL_INTERPS ...) approach correctly rejects 'bash.old' because _basename('/tmp/bash.old') = 'bash.old' which is not in _SHELL_INTERPS; this validates the membership-based approach is safer than a broad regex
acceptance_criteria.verification_command: python3 -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0, 'hooks/lib'); from bash_context_strip import strip_non_executable_contexts; import re; cmd = \"cat <<'EOF' | /tmp/bash.old\nrm /tmp/x\nEOF\"; out = strip_non_executable_contexts(cmd); assert not re.search(r'(^|[ \t;|&(])rm\s', out), f'rm incorrectly visible in: {out!r}'; print('AC8 PASS (body correctly stripped):', repr(out))"

acceptance_criteria.title: Existing tests still pass (no regression)
acceptance_criteria.given: The full test suite hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context.py exists and contains 30 passing tests at baseline (QA verified: 30 passed in 0.03s)
acceptance_criteria.when: pytest is run against the test file after applying all three bug fixes
acceptance_criteria.then: All tests pass with exit code 0; no previously-passing test may be demoted to fail; the test count must be >= 30 (new tests added by M4 are additive)

acceptance_criteria.title: +c form (POSIX-compliant variant) exposes rm
acceptance_criteria.given: The function strip_non_executable_contexts is called with the string "bash +c 'rm /tmp/x'"
acceptance_criteria.when: strip_non_executable_contexts processes the option word +c (POSIX-legal variant with + prefix)
acceptance_criteria.then: The returned string matches grep -qE '(^|[ \t;|&(])rm\s' — rm is visible as executable text; + prefix is accepted by the fix alongside - prefix
acceptance_criteria.fix_location: hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py:298 — the fix must accept tokens starting with + as well as -; the predicate should be: token starts with (- or +) AND not starts with -- AND 'c' in token[1:]
acceptance_criteria.verification_command: python3 -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0, 'hooks/lib'); from bash_context_strip import strip_non_executable_contexts; import re; out = strip_non_executable_contexts(\"bash +c 'rm /tmp/x'\"); assert re.search(r'(^|[ \t;|&(])rm\s', out), f'rm not visible in: {out!r}'; print('AC10 PASS:', repr(out))"
